intro: Hydrobryum vientianense is a species of liverwort, a small, non-vascular plant that belongs to the class Marchantiophyta. It is valued for its potential health benefits, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, making it useful in treating various ailments such as fever, cough, and skin conditions. The bioactive constituents of Hydrobryum vientianense include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, Hydrobryum vientianense is commonly used to make herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tonics, which are used to treat a range of health issues.
